title: Elixir Backend ↔ Agent-UI Disconnect Analysis
description: Comprehensive analysis of architectural disconnects between Elixir backend and TypeScript/React frontend

issues.title: Protocol Translation Gap
issues.description: WebSocket messages from Agent UI must be translated to GenServer calls, introducing latency and potential message loss
issues.recommendation: Implement direct WebSocket handlers in Phoenix channels with automatic GenServer translation

issues.title: State Synchronization Delay
issues.description: UI state updates are pulled on intervals while Elixir backend uses ETS for real-time state. Convoy and task updates may appear stale
issues.recommendation: Use Phoenix Presence for real-time state sync across all connected clients

issues.title: Git Bridge Latency
issues.description: simple-git operations run on Node.js side while Elixir uses its own Git state. Double-write pattern introduces inconsistency risk
issues.recommendation: Consolidate Git operations in one layer; prefer Elixir's Porcelain or Gitx for atomic operations

issues.title: Agent Lifecycle Mismatch
issues.description: Elixir's supervised agent model doesn't map directly to UI agent representations. Status updates can be out of sync
issues.recommendation: Implement explicit state machine for agent lifecycle visible to UI with proper terminal states

issues.title: Convoy Grouping Semantic Gap
issues.description: Convoy grouping logic exists in Elixir but UI shows different grouping due to TypeScript state transformation
issues.recommendation: Define shared convoy schema with validation at both boundaries

issues.title: Error Propagation Opacity
issues.description: Elixir errors are caught at gateway level and converted to generic HTTP responses. Original error context is lost
issues.recommendation: Implement structured error responses with error codes that map to UI error states

issues: Task status enum mismatch: TS uses TaskStatus enum, Elixir uses atom keys
Priority levels not synchronized during concurrent updates
Convoy status tracking happens in ETS but UI polls Git for updates
Task assignment events not propagated to UI in real-time
Agent mailbox messages not exposed to UI
Terminal PID mapping between Elixir and node-pty is implicit
